mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and signals policy easing, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive observations, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening labor demand and impending earnings downgrades, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the signal is unstable across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve, it indicates broad demand resilience in consumption and housing, which supports equity revenues and lifts S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform each series used alone in predicting 60-day returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial activity and profit expectations, so falling industrial production should lead negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if negative weekly changes in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the directional hit rate falls below 55% over a rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in PAYEMS combined with a falling UNRATE signals broad labor-market improvement, which should support consumer income and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down condition fails to beat the unconditional 60-day return rate, or if the signal loses significance after excluding one of the two labor series.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates fall and inflation pressure rises, which should compress risk appetite and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above FEDFUNDS
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-accelerating, low-rate episodes do not produce below-median 20-day returns, or if the joint interaction adds no predictive value versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 signals broad real demand weakness and lower expected corporate cash flows, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal fails to outperform a random baseline over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, weak retail sales together with soft housing starts indicate demand destruction is spreading across consumer sectors, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction is not stronger than either RSAFS or HOUST alone during tightening regimes, or if the 5-day hit rate for negative returns falls below 55%.
